picks.name: Dell Technologies Inc.
picks.thesis: DELL is down 10% from its 52-week high while earnings grew 34%/yr — a sentiment-driven pullback, not fundamental deterioration. Quality looks intact (healthy margins, durable revenue, long-term uptrend), and it's now at or below fair value ($421.63 vs $743.02, low confidence). Valued with DCF and Comps.

methods.name: DDM — dividend discount
methods.reason: Not meaningful: this is a growth-oriented company that reinvests and returns cash mainly via buybacks rather than dividends (yield 0.3%, payout 13%), so a dividend-only model understates it — DDM excluded

reconciliation.sentence: Only one method applied here, so treat the estimate cautiously (low confidence).

capital: Dividends: $1.5B paid in FY2026 — 17% of free cash flow
Buybacks: diluted share count down 9% over 3y
Leverage rising: total debt UP 6% over 3y ($29.6B → $31.5B)
change_mind: Price recovers above fair value (~$743.02) — the discount that makes it a buy is gone
Net margin (5%) deteriorates toward the 5% quality floor
Price breaks decisively below its long-term (≈200-day) trend
manual: Moat & competitive position — is the advantage durable?
Management quality and incentives (proxy statement, capital-allocation record)
Pending litigation or regulatory action beyond headline news
Upcoming catalysts — product cycles, guidance changes, M&A
profile: ✓ → multiple compression: price down 10% while earnings grew +34%/yr (sentiment, not deterioration)
